summary: iter210 fix is NOT bulletproof — CRITICAL TDZ bug pattern reproduced in production. Validated 15 URLs with `page.on('pageerror')` and `page.on('console')`. Confirmed 3 hard crashes with `ReferenceError: Cannot access 't' before initialization` on /profile (DisplayCurrencySelector), /settings (SettingsPage) and /games/quiz/challenges (QuizChallengesPage). Static AST sweep across src/ found the SAME broken pattern in 14 files: the codemod inserted `const FOO = getFoo(t);` immediately at the top of the function body, BEFORE `const { t } = useTranslation();` was declared on the next line — a textbook Temporal Dead Zone crash. The previous iter191 report was a false-positive because it only opened pages that did not contain these specific factory-call sites. CEO-mandated criterion (`0 ReferenceError`) is FAILED.

ui_bugs.issue: Line 47 calls `getCurrency_options(t)` BEFORE line 48 declares `const { t } = useTranslation()`. Page /profile crashes (ErrorBoundary catches: 'Cannot access t before initialization').
Line 576 calls `getSections(t)` BEFORE line 577 declares `const { t } = useTranslation()`. Whole /settings page crashes.
Function body calls `getTabs(t)` BEFORE `useTranslation()` declaration. /games/quiz/challenges crashes with TDZ.
Same TDZ pattern: getStatus_meta(t) / getSeverity_meta(t) called before useTranslation()
getStatuses(t) and getStatus_style(t) called before useTranslation()
getStatus_style(t)/getStatus_filters(t) called before useTranslation() — admin /admin/payments user view crashes when TxList renders

action_items: FIX THE CODEMOD `/app/scripts/definitive_i18n_fix_iter210.py` — when injecting `const FOO = getFoo(t);` into a function body, it MUST be inserted AFTER the existing `const { t } = useTranslation();` declaration (or insert a new `useTranslation()` call FIRST). Currently it inserts at top of body, creating a TDZ.
Re-run codemod on the 14 files listed in ui_bugs OR manually swap the two lines in each: move `const { t } = useTranslation();` up so it's declared BEFORE every `getXxx(t)` call.

Files to fix (14): pages/QuizChallengesPage.js:24, pages/SettingsPage.js:575, pages/admin/AdminErrorMonitorTab.jsx:38 & :254, pages/admin/AdsAdminTab.jsx:23, pages/admin/TransportPricingAdminTab.jsx:416 & :574, pages/admin/SupportAdminTab.jsx:33, pages/admin/PaymentsAdminTab.jsx:67, components/TranslateButton.jsx:30, components/profile/DisplayCurrencySelector.jsx:46, components/wallet/NowPaymentsDepositCard.jsx:46, components/transport/RideLifecyclePanel.jsx:37, components/transport/DriverKycForm.jsx:70.

Add an ESLint rule (or extend the existing `local-rules/no-module-level-t`) to forbid using identifier `t` BEFORE the `useTranslation()` destructure within the same function scope. This would have caught the bug at build time.
Re-test the 15 URLs + 14 sub-component crash sites after the fix. /admin sub-tabs need to be tested with the actual admin@japap.com account (Bob does not have admin tabs).
critical_code_review_comments: RCA: iter210 codemod inserts the factory-call assignment at the top of the function body without checking the position of the existing `const { t } = useTranslation()` line. The static AST audit passed (no module-level `t()`), but the runtime check (TDZ) was not validated by tests. Both yarn build and ESLint passed because TDZ is a runtime-only error.
The previous testing iteration (iter191) only validated specific deposit/wallet flows and did not navigate to /profile or /settings — false-positive PASS.

DisplayCurrencySelector.jsx is a strong example: lines 46-48 read literally `function DisplayCurrencySelector() { const CURRENCY_OPTIONS = getCurrency_options(t); const { t } = useTranslation(); ...}`. Trivial swap fixes it.

Recommend converting all `const FOO = getFoo(t);` patterns to `const FOO = useMemo(() => getFoo(t), [t]);` AFTER the useTranslation() line — both safer (memoised, recomputes only on language change) and unambiguous to future codemods.

success_rate.frontend: 12/15 URLs clean — 3 hard crashes (/profile, /settings, /games/quiz/challenges). Static analysis predicts 11 more crash sites that need admin-role / sub-tab interaction to surface.

test_credentials: bob@japap.com / Test1234! (math captcha solved programmatically). admin@japap.com not used yet — recommended for re-test after fix to exercise admin tabs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the codemod and re-runs it, re-test the SAME 15 URLs + login as admin@japap.com / JapapAdmin2024! and click EVERY admin tab (Users, Transactions/Payments, KYC, Pro, Referrals, Connect, Audit, Marketplace Disputes, Ads, Support, Transport Pricing, Error Monitor). Also navigate to /games/quiz/challenges, /transport (driver KYC form), /wallet (NowPayments deposit card with Crypto method selected). Use page.on('pageerror') AND page.on('console') filtering on `is not defined`/`before initialization`/`Can't find variable: t`. Multilingual (FR/EN/AR/SW) was NOT tested in this iteration because the language switcher would route through pages already broken — fix TDZ first, then re-test multilingual.
rca_of_the_issue: ROOT CAUSE: The codemod `/app/scripts/definitive_i18n_fix_iter210.py` injects factory-call assignments (e.g. `const SECTIONS = getSections(t);`) at the START of a function body, without first verifying where `const { t } = useTranslation();` sits. When `useTranslation()` is declared LATER in the body, the factory call hits a Temporal Dead Zone on `t` and the React component crashes (caught by ErrorBoundary, surfaced as `ReferenceError: Cannot access 't' before initialization`). REPRODUCTION: navigate to /profile, /settings, or /games/quiz/challenges as any logged-in user — the page renders the ErrorBoundary fallback and the console shows the TDZ stack. MITIGATION (suggested): in the codemod, locate the existing `useTranslation()` statement first, and insert the factory-call assignment AFTER it (or insert a new useTranslation() call as the very first statement). Alternative: wrap factory calls in `useMemo(() => getFoo(t), [t])` placed AFTER the useTranslation() destructure. Verify with new ESLint rule that flags use of `t` identifier before its declaration.
